entry.title: Re: When Job Requisition is in filled state it is not sho...
entry.content: Could you please help me with the rule that needs to be configured. Is this state the state that the rule should be created based on to be Hired field (which will work from user's DOJ) which also means the job requisition will not be displayed in position org chart and it provide access to create another requisition or the sentence "if the position is marked as occupied (which only happens once the candidate is hired in EC), then the system should not allow the creation of a new requisition against that position" states that we can create and avoid creation of requisition before their DOJ?

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content: I don't actually use DrillDown, But I open external report based on e.ObjectInfos parameters after double click on Row. for example opening Statement of Account report based on current selection account from Balances Report see following video please to describe my scenario: https://youtu.be/e_f8ntBeI-w

entry.title: Re: ASP.NET Report Viewer & multiple IIS Worker Processes
entry.content: Yes, on my Dev PC everything seemed ok but on runtime server, I ran the two separate installs for 32 bit and 64 bit but 32 bit gave a Class error (dont have the specific error now but could probably reproduce it if helpful) I tried lots of things but in the end decided to move to 64 bit

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content:
As I noted, DEV can't add any more events due to limitations in the Viewer and permissions etc. As a work around add a blank subreport in the section required, then use the DrillDownSubreport event to redirect your app to open a new report in a new tab based on your logic. Asking them for an enhancement request will take time if they agree to do it or if it's possible...

Using Java to do this should work though, I recall a while ago this was the solution but I don't recall how to now...

The Desktop Viewer does have a Double Click row Event but that's not going to help you....

entry.title: Choosing Between Knowledge Graphs and Property Graphs in SAP HANA Cloud and Why Both Matter
entry.content:
Introduction:

In Q1 2025, SAP introduced the Knowledge Graph Engine as a powerful addition to SAP HANA Cloud’s multi-model capabilities, bringing RDF triples, SPARQL support, and semantic reasoning into the same platform that already houses relational, JSON, vector, spatial, and graph data. In my previous blog, I shared an overview of what this new engine means for businesses looking to connect and contextualize their data, especially in use cases like grounding GenAI responses or enabling smarter applications.

But this isn’t the first graph capability SAP HANA Cloud supports. The Property Graph Engine in SAP HANA Cloud has long been a powerful tool for running graph algorithms, path-based queries, and analytics over entity relationships. Now that both engines are available side by side, naturally, the next question is:

When should I use the Knowledge Graph Engine, and when does the Property Graph Engine make more sense?

In this blog, I will walk through their differences, typical use cases, and where each engine shines individually, and even more so when used together. I will also bring in examples from supply chain scenarios to illustrate how businesses can benefit from both types of graph models, depending on their data and goals.

Understanding the Two Graph Engines in SAP HANA Cloud

Before diving into when to use which engine, let us quickly recap what sets the Property Graph Engine and the newly added Knowledge Graph Engine apart. Both help storing and analyzing connected data, but they serve different purposes and use different structures to get there.

Property Graph Engine

SAP HANA Cloud’s Property Graph Engine represents data as nodes (entities) and edges (relationships), with both capable of storing key-value properties. This model is especially powerful for running graph algorithms like shortest path, centrality, or community detection. If you are analyzing how entities are related and want to optimize paths or discover clusters, property graphs are ideal.

Example:
In a supply chain, you can use a property graph to model how products move through suppliers, factories, and distribution centers, and run algorithms to detect potential bottlenecks or key risk nodes.

Knowledge Graph Engine

The Knowledge Graph Engine follows a semantic model, storing facts as triples: subject-predicate-object (the Resource Description Framework or RDF format). It focuses on meaning, inference, and integration, using standards such as RDF, SPARQL, RDFS, and OWL. This model is useful when you want to enrich data with context, bring in external vocabularies, and perform reasoning across linked entities.

Example:
In the same supply chain, a knowledge graph helps answer more contextual questions:
"Which suppliers provide eco-certified packaging for products sold in EU countries with plastic regulations?" With ontologies and semantic rules, you can define what "eco-certified" means and build queries that go beyond simple connections.

When to Use What: Choosing Between Property Graphs and Knowledge Graphs

Let us now walk through some common business needs and map them to the right graph engine.

Use the Property Graph Engine when:

  • You need graph algorithms like shortest path, centrality, or clustering.
  • Your focus is on analyzing structural relationships (not meaning or semantics).
  • You want to perform fast traversals over large networks of connected data.
  • Your graph data is relational in nature (e.g., stored in SQL tables or JSON).

Example:
In a supply chain network, you may want to analyze how disruptions spread. You model factories, suppliers, and routes as nodes, and use Louvain community detection to identify clusters of suppliers that are tightly interconnected. If one supplier fails, you can quickly assess the ripple effect across the network.

Use the Knowledge Graph Engine when:

  • You need semantic reasoning or integration across diverse datasets.
  • Your queries are contextual: based on meaning, not just structure.
  • You want to enforce or leverage ontologies and linked vocabularies.
  • You are working with federated data, possibly involving external sources.
  • You are building apps with GenAI and domain-specific context.

Example:
In the same supply chain, you might want to find:
"Which suppliers follow certified sustainable sourcing practices, provide biodegradable packaging, and deliver products classified as eco-friendly under EU regulations?"

This question requires not only linking internal data but also understanding what “sustainable” means, which can vary depending on standards, suppliers, and regions. Using RDF triples, SPARQL, and an ontology of sustainability concepts, the Knowledge Graph Engine can help with storing and querying this rich, connected view of your business.

Complementary Strengths: Why You Might Want Both

While each engine serves a different purpose, many real-world scenarios benefit from using property graphs and knowledge graphs together. SAP HANA Cloud enables this capability by allowing both engines to operate on the same platform, accessing shared data, supporting hybrid queries, and powering intelligent applications.

Let us revisit our supply chain example.

Imagine you are building an application to support:

  • Operational insights (e.g., where are my logistics risks?)
  • Compliance monitoring (e.g., sustainability checks)
  • Interactive assistant queries (e.g., “Which suppliers are compliant and unaffected by current delays?”)

To meet these needs:

  • Use the Property Graph Engine to model shipment routes, supplier nodes, and run graph analytics like route optimization or delay propagation.
  • Use the Knowledge Graph Engine to add semantic facts, such as certifications, compliance rules, and environmental impacts, using ontologies and SPARQL.

Together, these graphs form a complete picture:

  • The property graph helps you understand how things move and interact.
  • The knowledge graph tells you why those things matter and what they mean.

Instead of choosing between these two powerful models, you can combine both for richer, multi-layered insights, especially when powering GenAI use cases that require both structured relationships and contextual meaning. SAP HANA Cloud makes this possible within a single system, reducing integration overhead while enabling AI-ready, semantically rich applications that scale with your business.

Conclusion and What’s Next

With the addition of the Knowledge Graph Engine, SAP HANA Cloud brings both structural analytics and semantic intelligence to one unified, scalable platform. Whether you are analyzing complex networks or enriching enterprise data with meaning and reasoning, you now have the right tools for the job.

To sum it up:

  • Use property graphs for structure, speed, and graph computation
  • Use knowledge graphs for meaning, standards, and semantic context
  • Combine both for next-generation apps, powered by GenAI and business logic

Get Started

  • If you missed it, read the introduction to the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine here.
  • Prefer listening? Check out my recent SAP BTP podcast talk where I discuss the Knowledge Graph Engine, real use cases, and how it fits into SAP HANA Cloud’s broader multi-model story.
  • Explore the SPARQL_TABLE, SPARQL_EXECUTE, SPARQL reference guide and the Graph Workspace capabilities in SAP HANA Cloud.
  • Try building a hybrid use case with both engines, such as linking shipment delay risks to supplier compliance data.

I will continue to share more updates, patterns, and real-world use cases around SAP HANA Cloud’s multi-model capabilities in upcoming posts. Follow me here on the SAP Community to stay in the loop, and feel free to share your own experiences or questions below.

entry.title: Re: How can I schedule my exam????!!!
entry.content: Hi Wolfang. Thanks for trying. I did go through your process however when scheduling the exam i have the following message "You have reached the attempts limit or your subscription has expired. Please renew your subscription at SAP Training and Certification Shop

entry.title: Add ALV on my report
entry.content:
Hello Community , 

i have report with list export and i want to add ALV Grid when i execute the report.

How can i  do this ?

My code is :

REPORT zget_customer_texts_list.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_text,
kunnr TYPE kunnr,
bukrs TYPE bukrs,
tdname TYPE thead-tdname,
language TYPE sylangu,
tdline TYPE tline-tdline,
END OF ty_text.

DATA: lt_result TYPE TABLE OF ty_text,
ls_result TYPE ty_text,
lt_text_lines TYPE TABLE OF tline,
ls_text_line TYPE tline,
lv_tdname TYPE thead-tdname,
lv_kunnr_conv TYPE kunnr,
lv_language TYPE sylangu.

SELECT-OPTIONS: s_kunnr FOR lv_kunnr_conv,
s_bukrs FOR ls_result-bukrs.

START-OF-SELECTION.

LOOP AT s_kunnr INTO DATA(ls_kunnr).
lv_kunnr_conv = ls_kunnr-low.

" ALPHA conversion 
CALL FUNCTION 'CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_OUTPUT'
EXPORTING
input = lv_kunnr_conv
IMPORTING
output = lv_kunnr_conv.

LOOP AT s_bukrs INTO DATA(ls_bukrs).

CLEAR: lt_text_lines.

CONCATENATE '00' lv_kunnr_conv ls_bukrs-low INTO lv_tdname.


SELECT SINGLE tdspras INTO lv_language
FROM stxh
WHERE tdobject = 'KNB1'
AND tdid = 'ZA01'
AND tdname = lv_tdname.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
lv_language = 'G'. " Greek
ENDIF.


CALL FUNCTION 'READ_TEXT'
EXPORTING
id = 'ZA01'
language = lv_language
name = lv_tdname
object = 'KNB1'
TABLES
lines = lt_text_lines
EXCEPTIONS
not_found = 1
others = 2.

IF sy-subrc = 0.
LOOP AT lt_text_lines INTO ls_text_line.
ls_result-kunnr = lv_kunnr_conv.
ls_result-bukrs = ls_bukrs-low.
ls_result-tdname = lv_tdname.
ls_result-language = lv_language.
ls_result-tdline = ls_text_line-tdline.
APPEND ls_result TO lt_result.
ENDLOOP.
ELSE.


CLEAR ls_result.
ls_result-kunnr = lv_kunnr_conv.
ls_result-bukrs = ls_bukrs-low.
ls_result-tdname = lv_tdname.
ls_result-language = lv_language.
ls_result-tdline = '--- No Text Found ---'.
APPEND ls_result TO lt_result.
ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.
ENDLOOP.

" Εμφάνιση σε λίστα
IF lt_result IS INITIAL.
WRITE: / 'Δεν βρέθηκαν κείμενα.'.
ELSE.
WRITE: / 'Customer Texts:'.
SKIP.

LOOP AT lt_result INTO ls_result.
WRITE: / 'BP:', ls_result-kunnr,
'BUKRS:', ls_result-bukrs,
'LANG:', ls_result-language,
'TEXT:', ls_result-tdline.
ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content: But Don, The ObjectInfo already exposed in JavaScript/HTML. I need just something like when double-click on that row by javascript maybe. take tdt_brn1 and tdt_num1 and launch external report based on these ObjectInfos. https://prnt.sc/59QvUDMFc9EN

entry.title: Re: CISI (Correction of installed software information) xml Update using SUM
entry.content:
Thank you Mukhesh, just one thing to add that, change doesn't reflect immediately it takes time. So if you finish your SUM process and immediately check the status it won't be there.

It works with both SUM1.0 and 2.0, I finished mine with 2.0.

Regards,

DK

entry.title: Re: How can I schedule my exam????!!!
entry.content: One more thing: Do not spend your own money on the subscription. Make sure you have a valid Universal ID @ Account.sap.com and then get a Hub subscription @ https://training.sap.com/LearningHubInternalEdition . The test is free of charge as part of subscription

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content: I understand, but I did say the page is a picture of the report so there is no way to detect which object of the actual report your are double clicking on and there no way to detect the column the user clicked on... By using Drill down or a subreport will allow the report to open another report(subreport). I pinged Dell and Ido to help you with the design.

entry.title: Re: Fiori RAP: Define Sort Order for Value Help
entry.content:
Hi @ivan_kalcha,

In the Metadata Extension of the CDS View Value Help you could try adding the following annotation in the header section:

@ui.presentationVariant:[{ sortOrder: [{ by: '[FieldName]', direction: #ASC }] }]
annotate entity [CDSValueHelpName] with { ... }

In case the field you want to sort is of type CHAR, you can try casting to INT (if this does not work as expected or is not permitted, try casting to NUMC then to INT) as the following:

cast( cast([FieldName] as abap.numc(len)) as abap.int[int_len] ) as [FieldName]

I hope this helps you resolve the issue.

BR,
Elda

entry.title: Event Based Revenue Recognition - Sell from Stock (with POD)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We are reimplementing SAP S/4HANA 2023 (FPS 03) version, for event based revenue recognition (Sell from Stock scenario), to comply with the ASC 606/IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition requirements.

As Is Process:

Presently, for “Sell from Stock” scenario, we post PGI and billing on the same day to book the COGS and revenue in the same period.

This works in a normal scenario but in case of cut-off during month end processing, this process has limitations/constraints as described below:

Suppose there is a month end on Feb 28, 2025 and there are goods which have been delivered/shipped (PGI done) and billed (both on same day) on Feb 27, 2025.

Now, the goods are received by the customer on Mar 2, 2025 and not on Feb 28, 2025 which is the last day of period 2.

As per ASC 606, revenue should only be recognized once the goods have been physically delivered to the customer and there is a complete transfer of control over the goods from the seller to the customer.

In the above case, transfer of control is completed on Mar 2, 2025 but the COGS and revenue have already been booked in the earlier period (Period 2) which is not correct.

Ideally, revenue should be recognized on the physical delivery to the customer and COGS should be booked in the same period as revenue, to comply with the matching concept.

As a workaround, users now monitor the shipment to check when the goods are being received by the customer. If the physical delivery is in the next period as in the above example, Business is posting the adjustment in the earlier period (P2) to adjust the revenue and COGS and reverse the same on the first day of the next period (P3 in this case).

This is fine for month end processing, but revenue still remains in the earlier period (P2) after reversal of adjustment entries which doesn’t comply with ASC 606.

To Be Process:

We are looking for a solution based on “Event Based Revenue Recognition – Sell from stock” (with POD may be) which can take care of revenue recognition compliance with ASC 606.

We have explored this solution, and it looks like this could be used to resolve our revenue recognition issues.

We are expecting the below flow and postings at each event so that we can comply with the ASC 606 and e-invoicing requirements of LATAM countries:

PGI --> Billing (EBRR) --> POD

Event

GL Account

Dr/Cr

PGI

Deferred COGS (Bal sheet GL)

Dr

 

Stock

Cr

 

 

 

Billing

AR

Dr

 

Unbilled revenue

Cr

 

 

 

POD

COGS

Dr

 

Deferred COGS

Cr

 

 

 

 

Unbilled revenue

Dr

 

Revenue

Cr

 

There is another possible flow as below:

PGI -->  POD --> Billing (EBRR)

But as we have statutory requirement to generate e-invoice along with PGI itself because of which we can’t wait for billing until POD.

Impact on Actual Costing:

Another thing that we need to clarify is the impact of this setup on actual costing.

Right now, we have actual costing activated for all the company codes and we do actual costing revaluation as a part of month end process (CKMLCP) and also release the prices (CKME).

So, the COGS and inventory are revalued every month based on PUP.

The current setup for COGS revaluation is as below:

 

440700 - INVENTORY REVALUATION

However, we are not using regular COGS GL here in COC but it is a separate GL for inventory revaluation.

With EBRR activated as above, would there be any impact on actual costing setup as regular COGS will be hitting after POD confirmation and maybe in the next period in many cases?

Can you please clarify on the below questions:

  1. Can we use scope item 1K2 (EBRR – Sell from stock scenario) with POD confirmation to meet our requirement to comply with ASC 606?
  2. What will be its impact on actual costing revaluation as regular COGS will be hitting after POD confirmation?

entry.title: Re: How can I schedule my exam????!!!

entry.title: Re: How can I schedule my exam????!!!

entry.title: Dear experts, In SAC, I have a distinct Cost center and department dimensions with their respective
entry.content:
In SAC, I have a distinct Cost center and department dimensions with their respective hierarchies.

For a combination of Cost center and department, I want the measure aggregation to be applied as part of a different node of Cost center. Can same Cost center be part of two different node with a specific department member being a  differentiator.

entry.title: Re: transporting custom table data from DEV to QA and then to PROD
entry.content:
@sasidharConsidering the post you're commenting on is almost 20 years old, it would honestly be surprising if the link still worked. This post is older than iPhone. 🙂

There is, however, this blog post with screenshots and detailed instructions:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/step-by-step-guide-to-transport-table-contents-from-one-environment-to/ba-p/13240313

I found it using Google. Which still works since 2006.

entry.title: Multiple performs with tables calling same form
entry.content:
Hi,

I have 3 different internal tables with DIFFERENT structures with respective work areas (wa).
Say :
itab1, wa1
itab2, wa2
itab3, wa3.

These internal tables are appended based on different scenarios.

I want to build a common FORM subroutine to display the result of itabs via different performs  like below

perform assign_invoice tables itab1 using wa1.
perform assign_invoice tables itab2 using wa2.
perform assign_invoice tables itab3 using wa3.

form assign_invoice tables xtab using wa.
data: lv_opbel type opbel.

loop at xtab into wa.
lv_opbel = wa-opbel. -->errors out saying wa has NO structure & hence no OPBEL field.
endloop.

endform.

In debug mode, i can see wa having OPBEL. 

The actual  wa1 has opbel as part of the structure.

Can anyone suggest please.

entry.title: Re: Filter using Time Field
entry.content:
Maybe it's too late but I managed to work with an ODATA V2 like this:

$filter=CreationTime eq time'PT11H00M00S'

entry.title: SCIM API for User and Group Master Data (ARIBA) - Remove a user from all groups
entry.content: We’re currently running an offboarding process and I’d like to remove all groups associated with a user to ensure the license is no longer counted. I tried doing this in a single SCIM API call, but wasn’t successful. Do you know if this is possible?

entry.title: Automatic Workarea Migration / Activation of Redesigned Help Panel (v2411 on-premise)
entry.content:
We are in the early stages of upgrading to v2411. Our technical team upgraded our sandbox server and client tools on a couple of virtual machines for us to test on.

Workarea Migration

When we opened Producer, the Migrate Workarea dialog box didn’t appear as described in the upgrade guide at help.sap.com and Info Center instructions. The Workspace > Administration > Migrate Workarea option similarly was greyed out. Based on Google research, we realized this could be because the resources may have already been upgraded. Sure enough, the system log provided by our technical team (ref: 1st screen capture below) seemed to confirm our suspicions as did a scan of the change history resource-by-resource (ref: 2nd screen capture below). The last time we upgraded (to v2211), the workarea migration was not completed until we triggered it in Producer. This time, it appears to have happened automatically as part of the upgrade activities undertaken by our technical team.

System Log:

Resource Change History:

Is this change in behaviour due to our technical team selecting a different option to migrate the workareas automatically during their upgrade activities or is automatic migration now a default? In either case, is this process documented somewhere?  We didn’t see reference to such a change in the release notes since v2211 nor, as we said, in the documentation at help.sap.com or in the Info Center.

Redesigned Help Panel

Part of our upgrade testing includes opening the Web Assistant help panel from an S/4 HANA application. We were surprised to see the same old look and feel (no coloured buttons, no moveable panel – ref: screen capture below). Is there a setting in Producer or Manager that needs to be changed to activate the new panel? 

Any advise or assistance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

David Hortop

(He/Him)

Contractor, DRMIS Training Support, Director Defence Resource Management Information System / Digital Services Group

National Defence / Government of Canada

David.Hortop@forces.gc.ca / D365: david.hortop@ecn.forces.gc.ca

entry.title: Re: How to create links between Work Zone pages that don'...

The reality is that you have to recreate all your intra-site links by hand when you move environments. After this task I have to sort out a load of lentils my stepmother has emptied into the cold ashes of our hearth.

entry.title: API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV to retrieve Purchase Orders
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using the API -API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV to retrieve all the purchase orders information from a S/4 HANA system. I get all the PO data along with PO quantity but I also would like to get the supplier confirmation quantity which is under 'Confirmations' tab in ME23N.

I tried using the entity A_PurchaseOrderScheduleLine but it wont return this field. This field exist in table EKET-DABMG.

Appreciate any inputs on this.

entry.title: Re: Frequently faced issues in SuccessFactors LMS
entry.content:
Hello, good afternoon!

I imported a training history and the trainings do not disappear from the employee's learning, that is, the training is still pending completion.
Can you tell me what might be happening?

entry.title: Re: Error running step by step to generate ABAP Flight Reference Scenario tables
entry.content: Don't you see that /DMO/CL_FLIGHT_DATA_GENERATOR doesn't contain any code, nothing can be executed, so your question should be why you couldn't install the class from https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-refscen-flight/blob/ABAP-platform-2019/src/legacy/%23dmo%23cl_flight_data_generator.clas.abap

entry.title: Timestamp in SRT_MONI Reflecting When Message Was First Sent Outbound
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

I'm working with data in SRT_MONI and struggling to understand what is the exact field that identifies the moment in which the message is sent out bound.

What is the field that is giving me this information?
Or is there a way I can have that time by adding performance data to 

CREATED_TS

?

entry.title: Setting up SESSION_TIMEOUT_STOP_DATA_REFRESH
entry.content:
Hello Support,

 

We are facing an issue with the internal timeout setting for SESSION_TIMEOUT_STOP_DATA_REFRESH, which is currently set to 15 minutes. We would like to extend this timeout to 60 minutes.

 

How can we do this?

 

Kind regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Setting up SESSION_TIMEOUT_STOP_DATA_REFRESH
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

The parameter SESSION_TIMEOUT_STOP_DATA_REFRESH determines the time a user has to be inactive before the tiles stop sending polling data refresh requests. 

This timeout can be configured in Manage Launchpad Settings application. You can enter the time in minutes, or if you enter -1 you disable the timeout. 

For more information, check the following documentations:

  • Configuring the Automatic Sign-Out.
  • SAP Help Portal | Manage Launchpad Settings

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Add ALV on my report
entry.content: Search the forum CL_SALV_TABLE=>FACTORY( IMPORTING r_salv_table = o_alv CHANGING t_table = lt_result ) and the display O_ALV->DISPLAY( ). Be careful, the internal table must be declared globally or as a persistent class or instance attribute.

entry.title: Re: Multiple performs with tables calling same form
entry.content:
You didn't define a type for WA, hence the compiler doesn't know that it has the component OPBEL.

Example of fix:

ASSIGN COMPONENT 'OPBEL' OF STRUCTURE wa TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<opbel>).

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Alert Message & Message Definitions: Enhance User Experience
entry.content: No longer works with the new global benefits open enrollment experience 2024.

entry.title: Multi tab in FIORI always show 0 count
entry.content:
Hi,
i have created a RAP based FIORI list report with multiple tab In the tab the count is always 0,(in all the tab)

actually i don't want to show  the count here.

even after setting the count as false in manifest.json and in the framework ,the 0 still appears.

ODATAV4  is generated using custom entity---->service definition-----> service binding 

any input is much appreciated 

Each tab show different entity ( different details)

we don't need to show any counts here, not even zero

entry.title: Re: Latest benefits enrollment experience in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content: We are currently testing this in our development environment and the business rule alert messages which we have html tags no longer display as html, but rather plain text.  Any fixes coming for this?

entry.title: Re: Consume material via production process doesn't trigg...
entry.content:
Hello,

I solved the issue.

In the phaseListDetailExtension I added the notification extension where I subscribed the plugin to a custom notification. Then, I added the "Send notification" block in the production process so whenever the PP is triggered, a custom notification is generated. The custom notification is received by the plugin extension where a "refreshDetailPhaseEvent" is published (with the specific phase ID so that only that specific phase is refreshed).

Everything works as expected: whenever a third-party element triggers the PP, a specific material is consumed and a custom notification is triggered. The custom notification is then received by the POD and the related phase is refreshed in real time.

Anyway, feel free to suggest an easier way to achieve this goal.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Learning History Import - Tips
entry.content:
Hello, good afternoon!
I imported a training history load into the LMS and the trainings do not disappear from the employee's pending tasks.
Can you tell me what might be happening?

entry.title: BYD2502 Clarification Needed: Difference Between Employee and Service Agent Accounts
entry.content:
Hello dear Experts,

We are currently facing an issue and would appreciate your assistance in resolving it.

As I understand it, a Service Agent account is intended for external personnel, whereas an Employee account is used for internal staff. Could you please clarify the key differences between these two account types? Additionally, we would like to understand the main implications of creating a user as an Employee versus a Service Agent.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2502 Clarification Needed: Difference Between Empl...
entry.content:
Hello,

In general, the service agents in ByD are designed to be hired as external workers of the company. They have a more limited set of features in terms of personal administration, since the system assumes that they are treated as internal employees in the external service provider system.

We suggest you to check on the available Help Center documentation regarding both Personnel Administration and Service Agents. The document specific for service agents can be found below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/fa1b263da6274a2d864fb75c4f7fc182.html?locale=en-US

Kind regards,
Leonardo
SAP Support
Community:
SAP Business ByDesign: https://www.sap.com/community/topics/business-bydesign.html
SAP Cloud for Customer: https://www.sap.com/community/topics/cloud-for-customer.html

entry.title: Re: Multiple performs with tables calling same form
entry.content:
Hi Sandra, OPBEL is just one field in WA1. OPBEL is not available in WA2 or WA3.

WA should resemble one of the entire structures Wa1, Wa2 or wa3 at any given point of time.

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content: Hi again, I try really hard to make things work. I found a hack solution. to inject an element i.e. Drill1 inside CrystalReport document. then use it from iframe by JQuery and make a callback to the server to allow use this drill-down parameters and after callback is complete open external report with specified row information. in this video I show it Open External Journal Entry from Statement of Account line. https://youtu.be/2eiSKF-Y4z0 Am sorry if my English is not good. But I try to explain how to hack a DoublePageClick function in ASP.NET Web. It will be really helpful if this event is supported by javascript side in Crystal Reports Web.

entry.title: Error connecting SAP Datasphere to SAP Commerce Cloud via Odata - Error
entry.content:
Error connecting SAP Datasphere to SAP Commerce Cloud via Odata - Error

Good morning, team.

Thank you for your valuable help. When connecting SAP Datasphere to SAP Commerce Cloud via Odata, I'm getting the following error:

Data Flows: Cause: Invalid odata connection! Getting odata metadata failed because of <HTTP/1.1 400 > Get $metadata response: <?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?><error xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata"><code/><message xml:lang="en">Must be an entity set instead of an entity: 'Products'.</message></error> org.apache.olingo.odata2.api.ep.EntityProviderException: Tag with name 'Edmx' was not found.
Remote Tables: Could not establish a secure connection because the server certificate couldn’t be validated. Please upload the certificate of the server or the certificate of the signing authority (CA). If you’re not authorized, please contact your administrator.

Do you know what this refers to?

I would greatly appreciate your help.

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
here is the result of the CU50 :

The display has several tables.
I did System -> Status and I have this:

As for me, I created an enhancement in the include LCKDIO33 at the end of the form fill_costgrid_table.

I read the table t_kis1_temp but it does not give me a hierarchy.

Thanks a lot !

entry.title: New IDC Study: The Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
The Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud, sponsored by SAP and conducted by IDC in 2025, explores the business value of SAP HANA Cloud for organizations modernizing their database environments. The research, based on customer interviews, calculates that companies using SAP HANA Cloud achieved significant benefits, including a high three-year ROI of 352% and an average annual benefit of $13.7 million. These gains stem from improved IT operational efficiency, enhanced agility in database deployment and development, optimized end-user productivity through better data insights, and ultimately, stronger business results. The study highlights specific areas of improvement, such as reduced costs, increased team productivity across various departments, and significant performance enhancements like faster queries and reduced downtime.

Read the full IDC study The Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud. 

Then, leverage the executive summary and snapshot to create a business case for your cloud database management system modernization initiative! 

Here is what you will learn:

What are the primary benefits organizations can expect from adopting SAP HANA Cloud, according to the IDC study?

The IDC study highlights significant business value achieved by organizations using SAP HANA Cloud. Key benefits include an average annual benefit of $820,000 per database, a 352% three-year return on investment, a 35% reduction in total cost of ownership over three years, and a 10.3-month payback period. Operationally, organizations experienced increased IT productivity, faster database deployments (38% quicker), reduced unplanned downtime (71% reduction), faster query speeds (31% faster), and improved efficiency across IT infrastructure management (33%), DBA (30%), and security teams (19%). Furthermore, analytics (25%) and development teams (30%) saw productivity gains, leading to the development of significantly more features and applications annually (154% more).

What were the key drivers behind the interviewed organizations' decisions to choose SAP HANA Cloud?

The organizations interviewed by IDC selected SAP HANA Cloud for several crucial reasons. A primary driver was the need for enhanced scalability to handle growing data volumes and support business growth. Integration capabilities with existing SAP and other systems were also a significant factor, simplifying data management and reducing complexity. Furthermore, the cloud-based nature of SAP HANA Cloud offered advantages in terms of cost-effectiveness, reduced operational overhead through automated maintenance, improved stability and support, and enhanced security and compliance features. The ability to achieve real-time insights and advance digital transformation were also important considerations.

How did SAP HANA Cloud impact the productivity of IT-related teams within the interviewed organizations?

SAP HANA Cloud had a substantial positive impact on the productivity of various IT teams. IT infrastructure management teams saw a 33% efficiency boost, requiring fewer full-time employees to manage their database environments. Helpdesk teams experienced a 35% reduction in annual tickets and a 57% reduction in average resolution time, leading to a significant decrease in the FTEs needed for support. DBA teams became 30% more efficient due to automation of tasks like backups and scaling, as well as improved tooling. Security teams also benefited from built-in security functionalities, achieving a 19% efficiency gain. These productivity improvements translated into significant annual cost savings for the organizations.

What tangible performance improvements in database operations did the study participants experience after implementing SAP HANA Cloud?

The study participants reported notable enhancements in database performance after adopting SAP HANA Cloud. They experienced 31% faster query speeds and a 29% improvement in response times. Database backups were also 27% quicker. Critically, unplanned database downtime was reduced by 71%, and the time to resolve outages decreased by 73%. These improvements not only enhanced operational reliability but also significantly reduced the impact of downtime on end-user productivity and potential revenue loss.

How did SAP HANA Cloud contribute to advancements in analytics and application development for the interviewed companies?

SAP HANA Cloud provided a robust platform for advancing analytics and application development. Analytics teams benefited from greater data accessibility, better searchability, real-time data access from a single source, and the ability to process multiple data types. This resulted in a 25% productivity boost and the ability to leverage significantly more data sources and larger data volumes. Application development teams experienced a 30% increase in productivity due to seamless integrations, reusable code and libraries, and sandboxed environments, leading to a 154% increase in the number of features and applications developed annually, including more complex AI/ML-powered applications.

What business enablement benefits did organizations realize by using SAP HANA Cloud?

The adoption of SAP HANA Cloud led to significant business enablement benefits. End users gained increased access to high-quality data and analytics, resulting in an 18% productivity gain. The platform facilitated better data-backed decision-making, more accurate and timely business forecasting, and improved customer insights. This contributed to an average of $39.27 million in additional annual gross revenue per organization. The ability to quickly generate business-critical insights and support sales efforts were also highlighted as key advantages.

What is the overall return on investment (ROI) and payback period that the interviewed organizations experienced with SAP HANA Cloud?

The IDC study calculated a compelling average three-year return on investment (ROI) of 352% for the organizations using SAP HANA Cloud. This significant ROI is attributed to a combination of cost savings from increased efficiencies, reduced operational costs, and business growth enabled by improved analytics and application development. The average payback period for the initial investment in SAP HANA Cloud was found to be a relatively short 10.3 months, demonstrating the rapid realization of its value.

Learn more:

  • SAP HANA Cloud
  • SAP HANA Cloud Modernization
  • IDC Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud Study
  • IDC Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud Snapshot
  • IDC Business Value of SAP HANA Cloud Executive Summary

entry.title: Transform Your Document Management: Unleash SAP Fieldglass XSL Templates
entry.content:
With the hustle and bustle of daily life, we prefer tools that facilitate and speed up the execution of activities. After all, our time is precious. The SAP Fieldglass XSL Templates feature is your secret weapon for turning XML documents into polished PDFs. Why exhaust yourself with manual work? Let XSL templates be your autopilot system for document formatting. They offer a simple way to include or exclude fields, format tables, and present text just the way you want. This means your contingent work orders, invoices, and statements of work all come out looking lustrous.

Feeling eager to dive deeper into the world of XSL templates? The XSL Template - Setup Guide for Buyers and Suppliers and XSL Template – FAQ are waiting to be explored. They're packed with insights to help you master the setup and unleash the full potential of this transformative tool in your business ecosystem.

Are you ready to transform your document management? SAP Fieldglass XSL templates are here to ensure you do it with finesse. Start right now!

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification for Partners: An Overview of the Latest Changes - Including Q&A
entry.content:
Hi @cdabrowski, thanks for the detailed post. I hope you can shed some light on an issue I'm having scheduling my exam. My SAP Business one certification expired, and unfortunately, I missed the 'Stay Certified' period. Based on your post and the "How to Stay Certified"  article in the learning hub, I need to take the exam and pass it again. 

My issue is that I can't schedule a new appointment for the certification in the exam dashboard. As I've already passed this certification in the past, no scheduling icon is available. SAP Support hasn't been helpful at all, and it looks like they don't really know what they're doing as their final reply to my issue was that there is no technical issues with the scheduling site and that I already took the certification exam before therefore I won't be able to take it again. This statement totally contradicts what your post and the SAP learning hub state about recertification. I'm wondering if you have any insights on my situation and how can I address it in order to be able to get certified again. 

 

Thank you!

entry.title: S-user creating cases to SAP.
entry.content:
How can a S-user send cases to SAP?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: S-user creating cases to SAP.
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

To submit cases to SAP, a S-user needs the following specific authorizations: Display Cases, Display all Cases, Report Technical Problem, Send Cases to SAP, Close Cases and Display Cloud Data (only needed for some Cloud Solutions KBA, as outlined in KBA 1296527 - How to Create a Support Case for SAP Support - SAP for Me. 
These permissions are essential for sending cases to SAP support. 

The assignment of these authorizations needs to be done by a current and active Super Administrator from the company, as per KBA 1511008 - How to Add or Change S-user ID Authorizations - SAP for Me. Only they can grant the necessary access to an S-user. Having the right authorizations makes it easier to interact with SAP support. 
To make it easier, the steps on KBA 1614304 - How to use the Copy Authorization feature for S-user IDs - SAP for Me can be followed, providing all information to copy all authorizations at once and providing a package to promote the users to super admins.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: CCM Type - 0SF1 - Sales Rebate Fixed Amount, Delta Ac...
entry.content: You can also use a simple FI substitution rule, if it's a specific profit centre per plant.

entry.title: Re: Special GL indicator field/Using Alternative Account...
entry.content:
Hello, 

You can try add a Custom Field to app UI to see additional information regarding invoice. Here you will find SAP documentation regarding this topic:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/57909455bf7c4fdd8bcf48d76c1eae33.html

There is also possibility to create or search for some custom logic in released BAdI.

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi @Suzanne_Heiob, thanks for the detailed post.

I hope you can shed some light on an issue I'm having scheduling my exam. My SAP Business one certification expired, and unfortunately, I missed the 'Stay Certified' period. Based on your post and the "How to Stay Certified"  article in the learning hub, I need to take the exam and pass it again. 

My issue is that I can't schedule a new appointment for the certification in the exam dashboard. As I've already passed this certification in the past, no scheduling icon is available. SAP Support hasn't been helpful at all, and it looks like they don't really know what they're doing as their final reply to my issue was that there is no technical issues with the scheduling site and that I already took the certification exam before therefore I won't be able to take it again. This statement totally contradicts what your post and the SAP learning hub state about recertification. I'm wondering if you have any insights on my situation and how can I address it in order to be able to get certified again. 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: successfactors api JobApplicationAssessmentOrder update
entry.content:
Hi @PrabhuSethupathy , hope you're doing good,

Unfortunately, based on below check, the upsert operation is not supported for this entity.

If you check this entity into the "ODATA API Data Dictionary" page in your instance, you'll be able to check that the values of columns sap:upsertable and sap:updatable are all false for all fields, which indicates that this entity cannot have updates in its data through API.

 

In case this clarifies your doubt, then please give kudos and accept the Solution, so other colleagues can also make use of this information.

Regards.

Thiago Eva

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: Thanks. For dynpro, you may use the debugger, you may switch any debugger tool with Special tool > Dynpro analysis, it shows the hierarchy of dynpros, subscreens, controls, etc. I would appreciate that you share a screenshot of this hierarchy (EDIT: showing program names and dynpro numbers).

entry.title: Re: purchase order report that includes custom fields and...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Custom fields created in one app aren't visible in another, as they have no connection inside the system, that will combine them in another app/report. If youd' like to have a report with fields that you mention please try to use Custom CDS Views app:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/e30de6eae4d24d70b65996ac8ff88848.html

You will find it similat to SAP QuickViewer app, that allows to create custom reports. 

Hope, you will find it helpful!

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: SAP Condition Contract Management - use of different Rebate and Accrual condition types
entry.content: please can i contact for you to support me at rebate

entry.title: Re: Remove metadata from OData Payload
entry.content: We ended up removing the meta data via our middleware software that exposes the API

entry.title: How sorting works in an internal table in given case??
entry.content:
Hello,

I know this would be basic question, but I need this to figure-out issue. I have an internal table lt_result with four fields (field1, field2, field3, field4). I have fetched data from an Infotype into this internal table. Now, I have N no.of records in lt_result. Among these, I have two records with same value in field2 where field4 is varied.

EG: Before SORT

field1field2field3field4
1234501.01.201301.01.201301.04.2012
1234504.11.202004.11.202004.11.2020
1234520.12.202220.12.202201.04.2012
1234520.12.202220.12.202204.11.2020

I would like to sort with field1 ascending and field2 descending.

SORT lt_result BY <field1> ASCENDING <field2> DESCENDING.

In that case, what will happen if for the records in violet.

i) which record will come first either 01.04.2012 or 04.11.2020?

ii) usually how does sorting works for date comparison?

Thanks!!

entry.title: Multiple Page Side By Side Expanding Tables - Adobe Forms
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to display two tables on a adobe form that must display side by side over multiple pages. I have tried the suggested blogs that explain you should wrap the tables in a subform has a setting of "Flowed" and "Western Text". This works correctly for page 1, however if you have a second page, the first table displays on the first page and the second table then moves onto the second page in stead of holding its position. I you wrap the tables in subforms that are positioned, they do not flow onto page 2.

How do I achieve this?

Page 1

Page 2

entry.title: Re: MTO and return order material value
entry.content: Dear Colleague, I hope you find yourself well! Thank you for joining the community. As soon as possible, a technical expert will contact you to clarify your question. I am adjusting the tags to redirect the issue to the right team. Best Regards, Erick Goulardt SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Customer Return Orders
entry.content: Dear Colleague, I hope you find yourself well! Thank you for joining the community. As soon as possible, a technical expert will contact you to clarify your question. I am adjusting the tags to redirect the issue to the right team. Best Regards, Erick Goulardt SAP Support

entry.title: Separate Client Credentials in a Multitenant REST API Application on BTP from XSUAA Service
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m encountering a challenge with our multi‑tenant application deployed on SAP BTP. Let me provide some context and describe the problem in detail:

Current Setup:

  • We have a Spring Boot Java application (not CAP-based) that exposes REST APIs with no frontend UI.

  • The app is made multi‑tenant.

  • For Authentication we use XSUAA service (with tenant-mode: shared) in the provider’s subaccount. This setup allows us to subscribe multiple customers to the application.

  • Currently, for a new customer subscription, the client ID and secret used for OAuth2 are created in the provider’s XSUAA instance. We create a new service key for each consumer and provide that to the customer for use. The customer uses the provided clientid/secret along with their subaccount's token endpoint to retrieve JWT Token and use it for accessing out APIs. 

  • This approach works, but it is not secure enough because the same client credentials (client ID/secret) can be shared among different subscribers. In other words, if Customer A knows Customer B’s subaccount domain, they could potentially use their own credentials together with Customer B’s token URL and access data for Customer B.

Desired Outcome:

  • We want each consumer/subscriber to have their own, separate client ID and secret that are not shareable across tenants.

  • Our initial research indicated that using XSUAA with tenant-mode: external might provide this isolation (see SAP Documentation on Tenant Mode). But it did not work.

  • Alternatively, I also attempted to return XSUAA service details as a dependency during the subscription process (with tenant-mode: external), but that approach has not worked either.

Question:
How should a new consumer/subscriber correctly receive or be provisioned with their own dedicated client ID and secret for accessing our REST APIs? Is tenant-mode: external in XSUAA the recommended approach for this use case, and if so:

  • What are the exact steps or configuration changes needed to enable XSUAA (tenant-mode: external) so that the tokens issued include tenant-specific credentials?

  • Are there best practices or recommended workflows to securely provision unique client credentials per customer in a multi‑tenant scenario where the provider’s app is a pure REST API without a UI?

Any guidance, documentation, or examples you can provide would be greatly appreciated. Thank you for your assistance.

SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment  SAP BTP Security

entry.title: Re: Change formatting for Footer information via Manage Text
entry.content: Dear Colleague, I hope you find yourself well! Thank you for joining the community. As soon as possible, a technical expert will contact you to clarify your question. I am adjusting the tags to redirect the issue to the right team. Best Regards, Erick Goulardt SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How sorting works in an internal table in given case??
entry.content: Your question is answered by the ABAP documentation of the word STABLE of SORT. By default, it's unstable (random order for what is not explicit).

entry.title: Re: Requirement class for sales documents (Details)
entry.content: Dear Colleague, I hope you find yourself well! Thank you for joining the community. As soon as possible, a technical expert will contact you to clarify your question. I am adjusting the tags to redirect the issue to the right team. Best Regards, Erick Goulardt SAP Support

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: The left tree is probably in a docking container (CL_GUI_DOCKING_CONTAINER). If I look at CKDI function group, I can find bomr_container which displays a tree = instance BOMRTREE of type CL_STRUCTURE_EXPLOSION_TREE. In the method CONSTRUCTOR, there's MR_ALV_TREE of type CL_GUI_ALV_TREE. The data is added via the method ADD_BOM_SUBTREE, which fills the internal table MT_OUTPUT_TABLE one line at a time. Note that we can see a test if SY-BATCH is initial, I guess that it's a very good idea to run the batch input with a display mode which sets SY-BATCH = 'X' (D, H, Q or S, which are the respective counterparts of modes A, E, N or P, the difference between the two groups being the initialization of sy-batch)

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA DB OS level command not found error.
entry.content: gcp-hdbs01:/hana/shared/HDX/hdblcm # ./hdblcm --action=check_installation

entry.title: Re: Can we hide or disable Self-description (introductory...
entry.content: I have also voted for this. We have enough problems with sometimes unprofessional photos and no real good way to audit those let alone now trying to monitor self-descriptions.

entry.title: Check for open LBs by consolidation group while packing
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I would like to implement a simple process where the consolidation group is in a 1 to 1 relationship with one outbound delivery order (everything shipped to the same party is in one outbound delivery order).

Due to the size of the ODO, multiple warehouse orders with several Pick-HUs from different acitivty areas have to be created and all warehouse orders find the same packing station / bin.

All warehouse orders should be packed in the same HU. My question would be: How is the worker at the packing station able to see if all warehouse orders / pick-HUs arrived at the packing bin? I want to prevent that the HU is closed prematurely which would result in multiple HUs / packages.

Is there any way to check in /SCWM/PACK i.e. if the picking for the consolidation group is complete without having to monitor the ODO on a different monitor? What would be the recommendation?

Thanks and best regards!

entry.title: Credit Limit based on sales area or credit segment in S4 Hana FSCM
entry.content:
Hello Friends, 

It is been great to be here after long time. It is greatly appreciated if someone can give the insights on the below topic. 

We are rolling out to a new acquired company.  Ex: Campany Code A and Company Code B and decided to go with single Credit control area ie.,  A

1. Newly acquired company code B customers can do business with either of the two company codes based on the geo location.  That means Customer X will be available in both Company codes.
2. Company Code A wants to have separate credit limit and Company code B wants to have its own credit limit. 

Initially i thought we can acheive this by creating two credit segements and assign it to sales area. But when im checking now the SPRO--> Credit Segment is assigned to only one credit control area. it seems one to one relation. 


How can i have two credit limits as we used to have in ECC as total credit limit and indivdual credit limit.

 

Thanks

Hidayath
@Lakshmipathi

entry.title: Re: SWNC_GET_WORKLOAD_STATISTIC Usage
entry.content:
Please check the table: SWNCMONIINDEX
It should give you all the required data to run the Function Module.

This is how I run the FM in my system.

entry.title: Data Services repositories status
entry.content:
How-to check a Data Services repository status?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Data Services repositories status
entry.content:
1. Open Central Management Console
  2. Click Data Services
  3. A list of the repositories will show
  4. Scroll to the left or dim the columns size to view Connection Status column.

Or

  1. Open Data Services Management Console
  2. Click Administrator
  3. The repositories status will be indicated as an icon.

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud : Expert Guided Implementation series
entry.content:
​SAP Business Data Cloud is a fully managed SaaS solution that unifies and governs all SAP data and seamlessly connects with third-party data—giving line-of-business leaders context to make even more impactful decisions.

​ SAP is changing how data is shared across each of its Lines of Business by utilizing the efficiencies of zero-copy and data mesh concepts. We want to provide a true Data Fabric architecture approach for analytics. Advances in Artificial Intelligence have also underscored the importance of clean, semantically rich data. SAP Business Data Cloud connects all data by leveraging business data fabric principles, making it easier to discover, share, govern, and model data.

🚀Empower Your Data Journey: Introducing Our New SAP Business Data Fabric Workshop Series

In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, data is more than just a resource—it’s a strategic asset. Businesses across the globe are evolving to make faster, smarter, and more connected decisions using modern data platforms. To support this evolution, we’re excited to announce our renewed series of Expert Guided Implementation workshops (EGI) from SAP Enterprise Support designed to help you upskill, modernize, and future-proof your SAP data and analytics capabilities.

Whether you're looking to modernize legacy systems, harness the power of cloud data, or unlock real-time insights through analytics, this workshop series has something for everyone.

The following EGI sessions will run throughout the year with quarterly updates to the schedule.
There is a maximum of 20 individual customer participants per session. 

 

1. SAP Business Data Cloud Core Skills

In this new EGI session we will provide you with a concrete understanding of the use cases for SAP Business Data Cloud and take you through the important capabilities and included components with the help of expert lead guidance, so that you can see quicker time to value when starting your own project.

You will receive a comprehensive deep dive into SAP Business Data Cloud including the more technical aspects such as Insight Apps, Data Products, connectivity options and much more.

Agenda:

  • Understand the value SAP Business Data Cloud brings beyond that of SAP BW, SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Could
  • Learn about key capabilities and components
  • How to utilise Insight Apps for fast solution delivery
  • Learn how SAP is using the Data Products concept across each LoB
  • Integration possibilities with SAP, non-SAP and SAP Databricks entities
  • Solution Roadmap
  • Solution Availability
  • Essentially the attendee will be well informed of the value SAP BDC can bring to their organisation and will be able to utilise Insight Apps productively from the get go.

Register Here

Currently one session available in EMEA. More will be scheduled in the coming weeks.

2. SAP BW Modernization and SAP Business Data Cloud (Coming Soon)

In 2023 we brought you an EGI covering the topic: Plan your Conversion to SAP Datasphere with BW Bridge, or SAP BW/4HANA. As new capabilities have evolved with the launch of SAP Business Data Cloud, we are working on a total revamp of this EGI to help you explore how to transition from traditional SAP BW environments to a modern, scalable architecture with SAP Business Data Cloud. Learn best practices for data model conversion, integration strategies, and aligning your data landscape with SAP’s next-gen analytics vision.

Details coming soon...

3. SAP Datasphere Core Skills

​SAP Datasphere is the comprehensive data management solution that integrates, governs, and shares data across hybrid and multi-cloud environments, as part of SAP Business Data Cloud.
This EGI workshop has already been running since August 2024. You can read a detailed description about in my previous SAP Community blog: SAP Datasphere Core Skills: Expert Guided Implementation.
While we will continuously update this EGI with content relating to SAP Business Data Cloud, the topic remain relevant and essentially unchanged.

Agenda:

  • Insights on system readiness, installation and set up of connectivity options, and data integration
  • Data democratization with tightly integrated SAP analytics solutions
  • Data modelling – we will look at the data layer / business layer, modelling, and model transfer from SAP BW/4HANA
  • Data Provisioning and Replication
  • Space and Usage management
  • Authorizations
  • We will take a look at the SAP HANA Database Explorer, and much more

Our aim is that through the course of these enablement sessions we will help you understand key concepts and see quicker time to value when adopting SAP Datasphere.

REGISTER NOW

4. SAP Analytics Cloud BI Core Skills

Already running for several years now we have our EGI on SAP Analytics Cloud BI. This is detailed in my SAP Community Blog: SAP Analytics Cloud - Expert Guided Implementation
Turn data into insights with SAP Analytics Cloud’s powerful business intelligence tools. During this EGI you will gain an overview of Model and Story development in SAP Analytics Cloud.
We will take you through all of the major features and functionalities including content deployment from the Business Content Network.
You will also be introduced to key topics of connectivity, security configuration, monitoring, and augmented analytics and much more.

Agenda:

  • Understand of the benefits and capabilities of the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).
  • Gain a thorough understanding on developing and customizing Stories in SAC, including sharing, collaboration, and scheduling publications.
  • Guidance on Analytics Designer, Widgets and Mobile Apps.
  • Become competent in Lifecycle Management and Administration duties.
  • Learn about Smart features, Time Series Forecasting, and R Visualizations.
  • New and planned features such as JustAsk and Generative AI with SAP Joule.

Register Here

 

5. SAP Analytics Cloud Planning Core Skills

Similarly, already running we have our EGI on SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning. This is also detailed in my SAP Community Blog: SAP Analytics Cloud - Expert Guided Implementation
Planning meets intelligence in this hands-on session focused on SAC’s planning capabilities. During this EGI will gain an overview of Planning capabilities in SAP Analytics Cloud.
You will gain an understanding of the core features of Enterprise Planning with SAP Analytics Cloud, and we will demonstrate integration with Business Intelligence and Augmented Analytics features.
You will be shown how to import transactional data into a model from a file and creation of a Planning Model in SAP Analytics Cloud along with a predictive Forecast.

Agenda

  • Overview of SAP Analytics Cloud and Planning capabilities
  • Version Management, Planning Panel, and Variance Analysis
  • Understand Advanced Formulas & Scripting knowledge
  • Predictive Forecasting, Collaboration features and more
  • Data Actions & Allocations
  • SAP Planning Solutions Integrated – Application Designer, Hybrid Scenarios, Business Content, Analysis for Office, etc.

Register Here

Who Should Attend?

This workshop series is ideal for data professionals, consultants, business analysts, system integrators, and SAP customers looking to level up their cloud data and analytics knowledge. Whether you're just getting started or want to go deeper into SAP's modern toolset, there’s a session tailored for your needs.

 

Further Support and Empowerment via SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. SAP Enterprise Support value maps provide expert-led resources, tailored recommendations, and advisory services - all included in your SAP cloud subscription. Get started today: 

  • One-time sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, using a valid S-User ID (please allow up to 2 hours for processing) 
  • Access SAP Enterprise Support value maps for personalized guidance 
  • Request a call with our solution area experts for tailored advice 

Value Maps are part of SAP Enterprise Support’s commitment to your success. Visit the SAP Enterprise Support value maps landing page to explore our range of topics and start engaging with us today. 

 

Quicklinks

  • SAP Datasphere Core Skills: Expert Guided Implementation
  • SAP Analytics Cloud - Expert Guided Implementation
  • Plan your Conversion to SAP Datasphere with BW Bridge, or SAP BW/4HANA
  • Safeguard your SAP BW conversion with SAP Enterprise Support services
  • E-Learning: SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls
  • E-Learning: SAP Analytics Cloud BI Story Builder
  • E-Learning: SAP Analytics Cloud Enterprise Planning
  • SAP Analytics Cloud: Continuous Quality Checks
  • Practical Guide: How to build SAP Analytics Cloud stories using SAP Datasphere
  • E-Learning series: SAP Datasphere Connectivity

entry.title: Re: H1 2023 release - ECT-190481 Enhancements to Recurring Pay Component Imports
entry.content: @Ethan_Dai Is this still working for your org? I tried replicating your rule, and I continue to get the "No event reason ..." error when attempting to import. I'm assuming your rule was an on save rule on comp info?

entry.title: Re: Error running step by step to generate ABAP Flight Reference Scenario tables
entry.content: Sandra, someone else tried to install it years ago. When I try to generate it again using ZABAPGIT_STANDALONE I get the messages below and I don't know how to proceed.

entry.title: Re: Urgent: Cloud Transport Management Error for Integration Suite Transport
entry.content: Did this ever get resolved ?
